channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The Now Bar is one of the best features in One UI 7 – and could soon come to your cheaper Samsung phone
item.description: A new leak suggests the Now Bar will be one of the features in One UI 7 that makes its way to everyone.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Head of top Apple Mac Linux distro resigns, citing burnout and slamming Linus Torvalds leadership
item.description: Hector Martin has resigned from Asahi Linux “effective immediately,” citing burnout and Linux community challenges.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Black IT leaders weigh in on what DEI means for IT
item.description: An increasing number of companies in the technology sector and beyond are ending or scaling back diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) programs despite research that has shown that DEI efforts have a positive impact on business outcomes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: DeepSeek Pauses Chatbot Downloads in South Korea Amid Privacy Concerns
item.description: South Korean officials report that DeepSeek has paused downloads of its chatbot apps while it works with local authorities to address privacy concerns.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Even as he's vowed to push the United States ahead in artificial intelligence research, President Donald Trump's threats to alter federal government contracts with chipmakers and slap new tariffs on the semiconductor industry may put new speed bumps in front of the tech industry.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASCAR 25, the new game from iRacing, will put Daytona 500 fans in the driver’s seat
item.description: The Daytona 500 is one of the more challenging races on the NASCAR circuit. The speedway is long and narrow, forcing drivers to be more aggressive. And the weather in central Florida doesn’t always cooperate. During the 2024 event, a deluge of rain had forced a Monday conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Callahan, Fast Company

item.title: Bay Area pioneer in conversational AI looks to the future
item.description: With improving technology and more applications for the use of artificial intelligence, AI could contribute $20 trillion to the global economy by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Juniper CEO: ‘I am disappointed and somewhat puzzled’ by DOJ merger rejection
item.description: Few would have predicted the U.S. Department of Justice would move to block HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. When it happened late last month, the two vendors and many industry watchers were caught off guard.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple’s emotional lamp and the future of robots
item.description: Pixar Animation Studios has an unusual logo. The basic logo is the word “Pixar.” But sometimes, an animated lamp named Luxo Jr. hops into the frame and jumps on the letter “i.” The lap exudes personality and represents Pixar’s ability to turn any object into a compelling character.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple has been charged with abuse of power by this German watchdog. Here’s why
item.description: The German antitrust authority has charged Apple with abusing its market power through its app tracking tool and giving itself preferential treatment in a move that could result in daily fines for the iPhone maker if it fails to change its business practices.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Archaeologists unearth the remains of a Roman basilica on the site of a new London skyscraper
item.description: Work to give London yet another skyscraper has uncovered traces of the city’s origins almost 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Memo: Musk rivals Trump as most controversial figure on political stage
item.description: President Trump may be only the second most controversial person in his own administration. Three weeks into Trump’s second term, Elon Musk has taken center stage — as big of a villain for liberals and Trump critics as he is a hero for MAGA conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: House Commerce chair launches data privacy working group
item.description: House Committee on Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) on Wednesday announced the creation of a data privacy working group as policymakers work toward a national privacy standard.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple’s Chinese AI problem (perhaps) solved with Alibaba
item.description: Reflecting the erosion of universality, Apple Intelligence will now be coming to China, but rather than working with a US AI partner, the company will use Chinese-made AI tech from Alibaba.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Yes, you can still upgrade Windows 10 PCs to Windows 11
item.description: Windows 10 has less than a year left before it hits its end of support deadline. Starting in October 2025, you’ll have to pay for security updates if you want to keep using Microsoft’s nearly-nine-year-old operating system.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Rust memory management explained
item.description: The Rust programming language shares many concepts with other languages intended for systems programming. For instance, Rust distinguishes between memory allocated from the stack and memory allocated from the heap.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel
item.description: The intelligence community has increasingly prioritized OSINT in recent years. Now, House lawmakers are creating a new subcommittee dedicated to the discipline. The post OSINT gets its own subcommittee on House intelligence panel first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IP Fabric expands network visibility, automation with platform update
item.description: As modern networking deployments across the cloud and edge become increasingly complex, it can be a non-trivial exercise for teams to get proper visibility.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ESA’s space telescope Euclid discovers ‘astonishing' Einstein ring
item.description: The European Space Agency's Euclid space telescope was going through preliminary tests in September 2023 when it captured images of an Einstein Ring in a nearby galaxy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
